properties.media:imagetext:
AA
For Your Safety
737-800
If you are sitting in an overwing window exit row please identify yourself to a crew
member to allow for reseating if: You lack the ability to read, speak, or understand
the language, or the graphic form, or the ability to understand oral crew commands
in the language specified by the airline.
Para su Seguridad
Si está sentado en una ventana de salida sobre las alas, sírvase identificarse con
un miembro de la tripulación para que lo cambie de asiento si: No puede leer,
hablar o entender el idioma, o forma gráfica, o no entiende las ordenes verbales de
la tripulación en el idioma especificado por la aerolínea
Pour votre Sécurité
Si vous êtes assis près d'une rangée à proximité du hublot issue de secours aile,
veuillez le signaler a l'un des membres de l'équipage pour qu'il puisse vous trouver
un autre siège si : vous ne pouvez pas lire, parler ou comprendre la langue ou le
graphique ou si vous ne pouvez pas comprendre les instructions orales de
Für Ihre Sicherheit
Wenn Sie in einer Ausgangsreihe über dem Flügel sitzen, melden Sie sich bitte bei
einem Crew Mitglied, damit Ihnen einen neuer Sitz zugewiesen werden kann, wenn:
Sie die Sprache bzw. die graphische Form nicht lesen, sprechen oder verstehen
können oder die mündlichen Anweisungen der Crew in der von der Fluggesellschaft
vorgeschriebenen Sprache nicht verstehen können.
Para sua Segurança
O seu assento é numa janela sobre a asa de fila de saída; por favor identifique-se a
um membro da tripulação para possibilitar um novo assento se: não falar, ler ou
compreender a língua, ou o gráfico, ou não entender as instruções verbais da
tripulação na língua especificada pela companhia aérea.
Emergency exits are often heavy, awkward to lift, push, pull, and maneuver when opening.
Because of this, and for the safety of all passengers, Federal Law requires that we only seat
qualified passengers next to exits. If you are sitting in an exit row, you may be asked to open
the exit and assist our crew in the event of an emergency. Please ask to be reseated if you:
• Have a physical condition which may not be visible which may prevent you from helping
• Lack the strength, mobility, dexterity, or balance required to open the exit and assist our crew.
• Do not think you could reach the exit, open it, and get out quickly.
• Believe the physical exertion required to open the exit would injure you.
• Are hearing impaired and could not hear commands from our crew.
• Lack sufficient visual capacity to perform one or more of the applicable duties without the
assistance of visual aids beyond contact lenses or eyeglasses.
• Are not able to give oral commands to others.
• Do not understand the instructions in this brochure.
• Have other responsibilities (such as a small child to care for) which would prevent you from
helping.
• Are less than 15 years old
• Do not wish to help in this way.
Federal regulations require all passengers, regardless of where they are sitting or
their physical condition, to review this card, for their safety.
Federal regulations require customer compliance with lighted information signs,
posted placards, areas designated as non-smoking, and crew member instructions
with regard to these items.
When finished reviewing this card, please return it to the seat pocket for our next customer.
PM0395 (Rev. 6/01)
